AC.name: Access Control
AC.description: Controls for managing access to systems and information, including account management, access enforcement, and least privilege.
AC.azureGuidance: Use Microsoft Entra ID for centralized identity. Apply Azure RBAC for role-based access. Enable Conditional Access policies for MFA. Use PIM for just-in-time privileged access.

AU.name: Audit and Accountability
AU.description: Controls for creating, protecting, and retaining audit records to enable monitoring, analysis, investigation, and reporting.
AU.azureGuidance: Enable Azure Monitor diagnostic settings. Route logs to Log Analytics workspace. Deploy Microsoft Sentinel for SIEM/SOAR. Retain audit logs per organizational policy (minimum 1 year for FedRAMP High).

AT.name: Awareness and Training
AT.description: Controls for security awareness training and role-based security training for personnel.
AT.azureGuidance: Leverage Microsoft Learn for Azure security training. Use Compliance Manager to track training requirements and evidence.

CM.name: Configuration Management
CM.description: Controls for establishing and maintaining baseline configurations and inventories of systems.
CM.azureGuidance: Define baseline configurations with Azure Policy. Deploy guard rails with Azure Blueprints. Monitor configuration drift with Change Tracking. Query resource state with Azure Resource Graph.

CP.name: Contingency Planning
CP.description: Controls for continuity of operations, disaster recovery, and backup procedures.
CP.azureGuidance: Configure Azure Backup for critical data. Deploy Azure Site Recovery for DR. Use Availability Zones for high availability. Enable GRS/GZRS for storage redundancy.

IA.name: Identification and Authentication
IA.description: Controls for identifying and authenticating users, devices, and services before granting access.
IA.azureGuidance: Enforce MFA via Conditional Access. Use certificate-based authentication for CAC/PIV. Deploy Managed Identities for service-to-service auth. Enable passwordless authentication methods.

IR.name: Incident Response
IR.description: Controls for incident detection, analysis, containment, eradication, and recovery procedures.
IR.azureGuidance: Deploy Microsoft Sentinel for incident detection and response. Configure automated playbooks with Logic Apps. Integrate Microsoft Defender for Cloud alerts into incident workflows.

MA.name: Maintenance
MA.description: Controls for system maintenance, including controlled maintenance and maintenance tools.
MA.azureGuidance: Use Azure Update Manager for patch management. Automate maintenance tasks with Azure Automation runbooks.

MP.name: Media Protection
MP.description: Controls for protecting information on digital and non-digital media during storage, transport, and disposal.
MP.azureGuidance: Enable Azure Disk Encryption for VMs. Use Azure Key Vault for key management. Enable Storage Service Encryption (SSE) with customer-managed keys for sensitive data.

PE.name: Physical and Environmental Protection
PE.description: Controls for physical access, monitoring, and environmental protections for facilities housing systems.
PE.azureGuidance: Azure datacenters provide physical security controls. Review Microsoft compliance documentation for datacenter certifications. For on-premises components, implement organizational physical security policies.

PL.name: Planning
PL.description: Controls for system security and privacy plans, rules of behavior, and information security architecture.
PL.azureGuidance: Use Compliance Manager for security planning and assessment tracking. Align architecture with Azure Security Benchmark recommendations.

PM.name: Program Management
PM.description: Controls for organization-wide information security program management.
PM.azureGuidance: Use Microsoft Purview for data governance. Leverage Compliance Manager for program-level tracking and reporting.

PS.name: Personnel Security
PS.description: Controls for personnel screening, termination, transfer, and access agreements.
PS.azureGuidance: Use Microsoft Entra ID access reviews for periodic re-certification. Automate account disable/removal on personnel termination. Configure lifecycle workflows for onboarding/offboarding.

PT.name: PII Processing and Transparency
PT.description: Controls for processing personally identifiable information with proper authority, purpose, and transparency.
PT.azureGuidance: Use Microsoft Purview for PII discovery and classification. Apply Azure Information Protection labels for sensitive data. Configure data loss prevention policies.

RA.name: Risk Assessment
RA.description: Controls for risk assessment, vulnerability scanning, and risk response.
RA.azureGuidance: Enable Microsoft Defender for Cloud for continuous vulnerability assessment. Configure automated MDFC recommendations. Integrate third-party vulnerability scanners for comprehensive coverage.

SA.name: System and Services Acquisition
SA.description: Controls for system development lifecycle, acquisition processes, and supply chain risk management.
SA.azureGuidance: Use Azure DevOps or GitHub for secure SDLC. Enable GitHub Advanced Security for code scanning. Implement supply chain security controls for dependencies.

SC.name: System and Communications Protection
SC.description: Controls for protecting communications and system boundaries, including encryption, boundary protection, and network security.
SC.azureGuidance: Deploy Azure Firewall for network boundary protection. Configure NSGs for microsegmentation. Enforce TLS 1.2+ for all communications. Use Azure VPN Gateway or ExpressRoute for encrypted connections.

SI.name: System and Information Integrity
SI.description: Controls for flaw remediation, malicious code protection, information system monitoring, and security alerts.
SI.azureGuidance: Enable Microsoft Defender for Cloud for security posture management. Deploy Microsoft Defender for Endpoint for endpoint protection. Configure Azure Monitor alerts for security events.

SR.name: Supply Chain Risk Management
SR.description: Controls for managing supply chain risks including acquisition strategies, supplier assessments, and component authenticity.
SR.azureGuidance: Use GitHub Advanced Security for dependency scanning. Implement SBOMs for software supply chain transparency. Review Azure service provider compliance certifications.

metadata.note: Azure service mappings are curated guidance — verify against current Azure documentation for your specific deployment.
